scope: Artifact-only local remediation readiness packet for IF09-FIND-001 S3.
candidate_change_surface: Canonical sync packet assembly logic responsible for selecting and persisting latest_completed_project_commit_sha.
Primary-ref verification logic that compares recorded project SHA against the verified canonical ref before packet acceptance.
Drift-artifact emission or fail-closed handling for SHA mismatch conditions.
Regression coverage for canonical SHA sync behavior and forbidden-surface preservation.
future_files_or_modules_explicitly_out_o: secret-bearing integrations or external-network execution paths
candidate or invalid findings outside IF09-FIND-001
preconditions_for_future_local_remediati: A future apply gate must name the exact canonical sync control to be changed before any code edit is authorized.
A deterministic verified-primary-ref selection rule must be documented before remediation execution.
Mismatch handling must be specified as explicit drift-artifact emission or fail-closed behavior before remediation execution.
Regression coverage for IF10-REG-001 at tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py must be prepared before remediation execution.
All real execution, patch apply, runtime, secrets, product, Bedrock, and revalidation locks must remain false until a dedicated future apply gate changes them explicitly.
required_regression_tests: tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py must assert that latest_completed_project_commit_sha equals the verified primary ref selected for the canonical packet.
tests/test_active_context_canonical_sha_sync.py must assert that SHA mismatch triggers explicit drift-artifact emission or fail-closed behavior.
Regression proof must confirm validator and sync artifacts preserve forbidden execution surfaces as false.
Existing validator flow must continue to pass after the future remediation proof artifacts are materialized.
required_before_after_evidence: A before snapshot proving the stale-SHA condition recorded for IF09-FIND-001.
An after snapshot proving the corrected canonical packet derives the SHA from verified primary refs.
An evidence link showing how mismatch custody is preserved before and after the future remediation.
A no-real-execution / no-real-apply attestation attached to the future remediation proof.
rollback_plan_documental: If regression coverage fails, rollback must restore the prior canonical sync control before any corrected packet claim is promoted.
Rollback must preserve any emitted drift artifacts and must not erase mismatch custody evidence.
Rollback completion must still leave validator pass or the future remediation gate must block without claiming success.
blast_radius_limits: The future remediation is limited to active_context_governance and canonical sync packet verification surfaces.
No runtime mutation, product behavior, Bedrock readiness, secrets, external network, MCP, RAG, memory-write, or real_apply surfaces may be touched.
No unrelated findings, candidate findings, or invalid findings may be promoted, reopened, or modified.
future_remediation_blocking_conditions: The candidate change surface cannot be mapped to a concrete canonical sync control.
The future regression test path or assertions for IF10-REG-001 remain undefined or ambiguous.
The future remediation requires apply, runtime, or lock inversion without a dedicated future apply gate.
Before/after evidence or rollback evidence cannot be produced without structural schema drift outside approved scope.
Mismatch handling still depends on silent SHA normalization.
